zEGeneric wrapper for read-eval-print-loops, a.k.a. interactive shells
[PEXPECT_PROMPT>z
[PEXPECT_PROMPT+c
REPLWrappera
Wrapper for a REPL.
    :param cmd_or_spawn: This can either be an instance of :class:`pexpect.spawn`
      in which a REPL has already been started, or a str command to start a new
      REPL process.
    :param str orig_prompt: The prompt to expect at first.
    :param str prompt_change: A command to change the prompt to something more
      unique. If this is ``None``, the prompt will not be changed. This will
      be formatted with the new and continuation prompts as positional
      parameters, so you can use ``{}`` style formatting to insert them into
      the command.
    :param str new_prompt: The more unique prompt to expect after the change.
    :param str extra_init_cmd: Commands to do extra initialisation, such as
      disabling pagers.
    Nc
utf-8
echo
encoding)
isinstance
basestring
pexpect
spawn
childr
setechoZ
waitnoecho
prompt
set_prompt
format
continuation_prompt
_expect_prompt
run_command)
selfZ
cmd_or_spawn
orig_prompt
prompt_changeZ
new_promptr
extra_init_cmd
4/usr/lib/python3.9/site-packages/pexpect/replwrap.py
__init__!
REPLWrapper.__init__c
expect
sendline)
REPLWrapper.set_prompt
timeout
async_)
expect_exactr
REPLWrapper._expect_promptc
Send a command to the REPL, wait for and return output.
        :param str command: The command to send. Trailing newlines are not needed.
          This should be a complete block of input that will trigger execution;
          if a continuation prompt is found after sending input, :exc:`ValueError`
          will be raised.
        :param int timeout: How long to wait for the next prompt. -1 means the
          default from the :class:`pexpect.spawn` object (default 30 seconds).
          None means to wait indefinitely.
        :param bool async_: On Python 3.4, or Python 3.3 with asyncio
          installed, passing ``async_=True`` will make this return an
          :mod:`asyncio` Future, which you can yield from to get the same
          result that this method would normally give directly.
        
No command was given
repl_run_command_asyncr
z2Continuation prompt found - input was incomplete:
splitlines
endswith

ValueErrorZ

liner
REPLWrapper.run_command)

PEXPECT_PROMPT
PEXPECT_CONTINUATION_PROMPTr
pythonc
z>Start a Python shell and return a :class:`REPLWrapper` object.z
>>> z(import sys; sys.ps1={0!r}; sys.ps2={1!r})
bashc
z<Start a bash shell and return a :class:`REPLWrapper` object.z	bashrc.shz
